title: Selecting optimal unrestricted Hartree-Fock trial wavefunctions for phaseless auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo: Accuracy and limitations in modeling three iron-sulfur clusters
authors: Don Danilov, Brad Ganoe, Leon Otis, Zhi Gong, Zixiang Lu, James Shee
abstract: Phaseless auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo (ph-AFQMC) has emerged as a promising electronic structure method for correlated electronic systems. However, the quality of its predictions depends critically on the choice of trial wavefunction, and it is not obvious how to make an optimal choice especially for strongly correlated states of large systems. Mean-field wavefunctions are compelling trial wavefunction candidates as they map directly to chemical concepts and can be obtained with $O(N^4)$ cost. Yet in the strongly correlated regime one faces a symmetry dilemma and the existence of multiple nearly-degenerate solutions. In this work we investigate active space models of [2Fe-2S]$^{2+}$, mixed-valent [4Fe-4S]$^{2+}$, and [4Fe-4S]$^{4+}$ and explore the sensitivity of ph-AFQMC to the choice of unrestricted Hartree-Fock trial wavefunction. We find that chemical properties and physical symmetries, rather than the variational energy, ought to guide the choice of mean-field trial for ph-AFQMC (or reference state for coupled cluster models), and show that surprisingly accurate ground-state energies for these systems can be obtained. However, in all cases we find a rapidly vanishing overlap between the stochastic wavefunction and the UHF trial, indicating that the trials are suboptimal importance functions. By analogy to a similar situation in the stretched helium dimer cation, we show how this sampling bias pushes ph-AFQMC towards artificially negative energies, which evidently can be compensated for by the phaseless bias in certain cases.
summary: This paper investigates how the choice of mean-field trial wavefunctions affects the accuracy of ph-AFQMC in transition metal clusters. It demonstrates that while energy-minimizing trials are not always best, selecting trials that preserve physical symmetries can yield surprisingly accurate results despite significant sampling bias.
main_problem: Determining how to select optimal Unrestricted Hartree-Fock (UHF) trial wavefunctions for phaseless auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo (ph-AFQMC) to minimize bias in strongly correlated systems.
main_result: The study finds that choosing trial wavefunctions based on physical symmetries and chemical properties (like spin density) is more effective than minimizing variational energy, though the trial states remain suboptimal importance functions that can lead to sampling bias.
method: Phaseless auxiliary-field quantum Monte Carlo (ph-AFQMC) benchmarked against DMRG, FCI, and CCSD(T) using spin-density metrics to evaluate UHF trial states.
model_or_system: Three iron-sulfur (Fe-S) active space models ([2Fe-2S]2+, [4Fe-4S]2+, and [4Fe-4S]4+) and a stretched helium dimer cation (He2+) used as an analogy for sampling bias.
key_observables: Ground-state energies, atomic spin densities, Mulliken spin density, Spin Density Metric (SDM), overlap between stochastic and trial wavefunctions, and local energy fluctuations.
important_parameters: Imaginary-time step (0.005), walker populations (1024-2048), and Cholesky decomposition threshold (10^-12).
main_assumptions: The phaseless constraint is assumed to be unbiased if the trial wavefunction is an exact eigenstate; the study explores the breakdown of this assumption.
figures_summary: The figures show ph-AFQMC energy errors relative to DMRG/FCI as a function of the Spin Density Metric, energy evolution over imaginary time, and the decay of wavefunction overlap.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the ph-AFQMC problem, presents investigations of specific Fe-S clusters, uses a He2+ dissociation model to explain the mechanism of sampling bias, and concludes with implications for trial wavefunction design.
why_interesting: It provides deep insight into the systematic errors and sampling pathologies (like overcorrelation) that occur when using mean-field importance functions in many-body Monte Carlo simulations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Cluster geometry (left), idealized spin configuration (middle), and corresponding reference spin density metric (right) for [2Fe–2S]2+.
Figure 2: Same as Figure 1 but for [4Fe–4S]2+ (top) and [4Fe–4S]4+ (bottom) states.
Figure 3: Deviations of the ph-AFQMC-UHF and UCCSD(T) energies from the DMRG reference, as a function of the deviation of the UHF trial/reference energy from DMRG.

Figure 4: ph-AFQMC-UHF and UCCSD(T) energies as a function of the UHF trial/reference energy. The three reference theoretical best estimates are ERef[++– –] = −327.247062 ± 0.000984, ERef[+–+–]= −327.245289 ± 0.000733, ERef[+– –+]= −327.248199 ± 0.001820 from Zhai et al,32 wherein the 3 solutions are referred to as BS1, BS2, and BS3 respectively (the error bars are omitted for clarity).
Figure 5: Deviations of the ph-AFQMC-UHF and UCCSD(T) energies from the theoretical best estimate32
